refined_fr.refined_fr: Provide detailed worksite area analysis including terrain mapping, obstacle identification, and work-zone representation to support planning and execution.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; decomposing into specific sub-functions clarifies scope and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify each sub-FR by confirming the generation and accuracy of terrain maps, obstacle lists, and work-zone models against ground truth data.
sub_frs: Generate terrain map of the worksite area
Identify and locate obstacles within the worksite
Represent designated work-zones within the worksite model

candidate_dps: Use LIDAR and camera sensor fusion for terrain and obstacle mapping
Apply geospatial data processing algorithms for work-zone representation
Integrate sensor data into a unified digital worksite model
selected_dp.selected_dp: Use LIDAR and camera sensor fusion for terrain and obstacle mapping

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically addresses the FR with minimal coupling to unrelated functions; sensor fusion is a cohesive approach.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability and reliability of LIDAR and camera sensors in all environmental conditions
Risk of incomplete obstacle detection due to sensor occlusion or failure
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding redundancy or alternative sensing methods to mitigate sensor failure risks.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Central processing and data fusion for worksite mapping and modeling

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw sensor data streams from Vehicle sensors

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends processed worksite model data to planning and execution modules

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as NGHE Solution centralizes mapping functions; low diffusion risk.
Vehicle provides raw data but does not process mapping; high diffusion if mapping logic placed here.
Not relevant for mapping; poor containment and high diffusion risk.
Safety Services may consume mapping data but should not implement mapping logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Mapping and modeling logic is best centralized within NGHE Solution to minimize diffusion and maximize ownership clarity.
containment_risks: Potential performance bottleneck if NGHE Solution becomes overloaded with data processing
Dependency on reliable Telemetry interface for timely data delivery
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor processing load and consider modularizing mapping sub-functions if performance issues arise.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly satisfies the refined FR with clear traceability and minimal coupling.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk due to centralized implementation in NGHE Solution
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support NGHE Solution as the best IM boundary for this DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by testing the NGHE Solution's output models against ground truth data.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of mapping functions to NGHE Solution with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add contingency for sensor failure in DP assumptions
Plan for performance monitoring of NGHE Solution processing load

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment are consistent, well-contained, and verifiable with no major risks requiring immediate rework.
next_level_sub_frs: Generate terrain map of the worksite area
Identify and locate obstacles within the worksite
Represent designated work-zones within the worksite model

revise.why: Incorporate sensor redundancy and failure mitigation strategies in the next refinement level.

assumptions: Availability and reliability of LIDAR and camera sensors
Timely and accurate Telemetry data delivery
open_risks: Sensor occlusion or failure impacting mapping accuracy
Potential processing bottlenecks in NGHE Solution
evidence_needed: Sensor performance data under varied environmental conditions
Processing load benchmarks for NGHE Solution
